message:
feat(abi): port statmount/listmount + finit_module + quotactl_fd + map_shadow_stack + bpf + kexec_load + kexec_file_load (bd-dyh3u)

Eight more modern Linux libc syscall wrappers, all forwarding via
libc::syscall and bridging errno through raw_syscall_with_errno or
raw_syscall_with_errno_long depending on the kernel return type:

  - statmount(*req, *out, bufsize, flags) (Linux 6.8+, syscall 457)
  - listmount(*req, *mnt_ids, count, flags) (Linux 6.8+, syscall 458)
  - finit_module(fd, *param_values, flags) (SYS_finit_module=313)
  - quotactl_fd(fd, cmd, id, *addr) (Linux 5.14+, SYS_quotactl_fd=443)
  - map_shadow_stack(addr, size, flags) (Linux 6.6+, syscall 453,
    x86_64 CET) -> long
  - bpf(cmd, *attr, size) (SYS_bpf=321)
  - kexec_load(entry, nr_segments, *segments, flags)
    (SYS_kexec_load=246) -> long
  - kexec_file_load(kernel_fd, initrd_fd, cmdline_len, *cmdline,
    flags) (SYS_kexec_file_load=320) -> long

statmount/listmount/map_shadow_stack are not yet in libc 0.2.185, so
their syscall numbers (457/458/453) are embedded as locals.

NULL-pointer guards return EFAULT for the cases where the kernel would
otherwise produce undefined behavior or a confusing errno. Opaque
*c_void pointers throughout for the kernel structs whose layouts are
version-specific.

Tests: 10 abi tests covering EFAULT NULL guards, invalid-fd /
unprivileged-caller errno paths (EBADF / EPERM / EINVAL / ENOSYS), and
the size==0+NULL/positive-size+NULL fork in bpf. All accept the broad
set of kernel-version / privilege fallbacks since the calls genuinely
need root/privilege to succeed for real on a sandbox.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Verify with focused rch test/check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T05:43:39.083290831Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T05:47:15.873717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T05:47:15.871683250Z","close_reason":"Implemented executable poll_ops fixture coverage; verified with rch test/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","poll","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-du4q6","title":"PORT: mergesort + heapsort (BSD libc sort variants)","description":"BSD libc mergesort(base, nmemb, size, compar) and heapsort(base, nmemb, size, compar) — sort variants of qsort with the same signature except they return int (0 on success, -1 with errno on failure). mergesort is STABLE (preserves input order for equal elements); heapsort is NOT stable but uses no auxiliary storage. Both are ~50 years old, present in libbsd 0.0/0.3, used by mtree, ls -t, sort -s, and any tool that needs stability without writing its own merge sort. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::sort::{mergesort, heapsort} that operate on byte slices via index permutation (so the existing qsort comparator-callback infrastructure works unchanged) + abi shims that return 0/-1 with ENOMEM on alloc failure (mergesort) or EINVAL on bad inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:23:10.898281655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:32.846694117Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:32.844274737Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdlib::sort {mergesort (stable, copy-out + timsort), heapsort (in-place max-heap)} + stdlib_abi shims with EINVAL/ENOMEM mapping; 13+9 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dvr22","title":"[HIGH] fuzz gap: signal/socket/fortify/setjmp/mmap/dlfcn/pthread/c11threads ABI modules are unfuzzed (mmap now partially covered by fuzz_mmap)","description":"## Motivation

/testing-fuzzing skill discovery pass on crates/frankenlibc-abi/src enumerated 3646 `pub unsafe extern "C" fn` symbols across 38 modules and cross-referenced against the 30 fuzz bins registered in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml. The CVE-class gap is concentrated in 8 modules that currently have **no** fuzz target:

| Priority | Module | Symbols | CVE class |
|---------|--------|---------|----------|
| 1 | mmap | 11 | memory-safety (UAF / OOB / W^X bypass) — **covered by fuzz_mmap in same PR** |
| 2 | signal | 29 | handler setup races, async-signal-safety, privilege escalation |
| 3 | socket | 18 | network attack surface, kernel ABI complexity |
| 4 | fortify | 79 | `__*_chk` overflow-detection wrappers — if broken, they silently fail to protect |
| 5 | setjmp | 12 | control-flow integrity, sigsetjmp edge cases |
| 6 | dlfcn | 7 | dlopen/dlsym attack surface |
| 7 | pthread | 141 | concurrency (mutex/cond/rwlock/barrier) — best done as TSan campaign |
| 8 | c11threads | 21 | C11 threading (thrd_/mtx_/cnd_) — same pattern as pthread |

## Status of priority-1 (mmap)

Delivered in the same push: `crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_mmap.rs` — stateful harness with a live-mapping arena, 7 ops (Map/Unmap/Protect/Sync/Advise/Remap/MarkStaleInPlace), guard-byte sentinels on writable mappings, alignment invariant on mmap/mremap return addresses, and the use-after-unmap contract (every op on a Stale slot must return -1 / MAP_FAILED). The harness intentionally exercises PROT_EXEC / RWX requests against the hardened-mode membrane to cover the expected refuse-or-clamp path. Registered as `fuzz_mmap` bin. Validation: cargo build + cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings both clean.

## Fix direction for the remaining 7

Each should follow the pattern already established by the 5 fuzz targets cc-libc shipped earlier this session (fuzz_printf_adversarial, fuzz_pathname, fuzz_env, fuzz_sched, fuzz_pidfd_timer):

- Stateful archetype with a handle table where applicable (signal → signal handler slots; socket → sockfds; pthread → thread / mutex / cond handles; setjmp → jmp_buf storage slots).
- Return-code contract + lifecycle invariant (stale handle → must fail).
- Direct syscall differential where possible (signal, socket via libc::syscall). For pthread/c11threads, use a BTreeMap / counter shadow model because the ABI deviates from raw syscall shape.
- Safety bounds: signal restricted to {0, SIGWINCH, SIGURG} the way fuzz_pidfd_timer does; socket in PF_LOCAL with SOCK_DGRAM + AT_EMPTY_PATH style so no real network traffic.
- TSan-specific campaign for pthread/c11threads; run with `RUSTFLAGS=-Zsanitizer=thread`.

## Score priority

Per /testing-fuzzing skill, score = (untrusted-input) * (complexity) * (unsafe/native) * (prior CVEs). The mmap surface scores highest because of 40+ years of mmap/munmap/mprotect CVEs (CVE-2017-1000405 ptrace race, CVE-2016-5195 'Dirty COW' mprotect interaction, CVE-2023-40283 vma-use-after-free). Signal is second (CVE-2020-14386 af_packet, CVE-2021-44733 tee), fortify third (CVE-2023-4911 'Looney Tunables' via __stack_chk_* interaction).

## Exit criteria

One child bead per remaining module, each:
- Delivers a fuzz target in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/
- Registers it in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml
- Achieves ≥1000 exec/s (skill Hard Rule 1) or, for stateful harnesses, ≥100 exec/s
- Cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings clean
- Seed corpus committed (exclude artifacts per existing .gitignore)
","notes":"Fuzz coverage delivered for all 8 priority modules + 4 pthread sub-targets: fuzz_mmap, fuzz_signal, fuzz_socket, fuzz_fortify, fuzz_setjmp, fuzz_dlfcn, fuzz_pthread_mutex, fuzz_pthread_cond, fuzz_pthread_keys, fuzz_pthread_rwlock, fuzz_pthread_sync_misc, fuzz_c11threads. All 12 sources present + registered in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml. Verified via rch full build (exit 0, 62s remote) + fuzz_signal 30s smoke (2.4M runs, ~77k exec/s, 0 crashes). fuzz_xdr added later in this session covers the 9th high-CVE surface (SunRPC XDR) — found and fixed bd-bhqcn, bd-lc5ss, bd-xpgak in that target.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:23:16.085325702Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-23T02:33:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T02:33:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","full-review","fuzz","syscall"]}
+{"id":"bd-dyh3u","title":"PORT: statmount/listmount + finit_module + quotactl_fd + map_shadow_stack + bpf + kexec_load + kexec_file_load (8 modern Linux syscalls)","description":"Add 8 modern Linux libc syscall wrappers, all forwarding via libc::syscall + raw_syscall_with_errno{,_long} bridges. (1) statmount(*req, *out, bufsize, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 457) — query a single mount by ID into struct statmount. (2) listmount(*req, *out, count, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 458) — enumerate child mount IDs into a buffer. (3) finit_module(fd, *args, flags) -> int (SYS_finit_module=313) — load a kernel module from an open file descriptor. (4) quotactl_fd(fd, cmd, id, *addr) -> int (Linux 5.14+, SYS_quotactl_fd=443) — like quotactl but takes an open fd instead of a block-device path. (5) map_shadow_stack(addr, size, flags) -> long (Linux 6.6+, syscall 453) — allocate a CET shadow stack on x86_64. (6) bpf(cmd, *attr, size) -> int (SYS_bpf=321) — universal BPF interface. (7) kexec_load(entry, nr_segments, *segments, flags) -> long (SYS_kexec_load=246) — load a new kernel image for kexec reboot. (8) kexec_file_load(kernel_fd, initrd_fd, cmdline_len, *cmdline, flags) -> long (SYS_kexec_file_load=320) — same but from open fds. NULL-pointer guards where applicable; opaque pointers for kernel structs whose layouts are version-specific.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:32.663108317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:32.663108317Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dyrgj","title":"PORT: dehumanize_number (NetBSD human-readable size parser)","description":"NetBSD dehumanize_number(str, *size_out) parses a C string of optional whitespace + optional sign + digits + optional one-char suffix (bBkKmMgGtTpPeE for 1024^k multipliers, where b/B = 1) into an int64_t. Returns 0 on success (writes through *size), -1 with errno = EINVAL for malformed input or ERANGE for arithmetic overflow. Used by NetBSD ftpd/mtree/dd-conv and other tools for human-readable size limits in config files. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::dehumanize_number::parse returning Result<i64, DehumanizeError> with Invalid/Overflow variants + thin abi shim that maps the error categories to errno + writes the value through the caller pointer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:22:44.076867837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:26:55.373009488Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:26:55.371160026Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdlib::dehumanize_number + stdlib_abi shim; 28+10 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e2ct","title":"syscall: close_range invalid flags on reversed bounds should still return EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for close_range(5, 4, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags plus reversed bounds stay in the exact EINVAL bucket.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:32:32.686596433Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:43:27.336651943Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:43:27.334571755Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e3y8","title":"wordexp parity: quote-aware badchar and command-substitution semantics","description":"Background:
- `wordexp` is marked Implemented, but current parsing is too naive about quoting/escaping context.
- Host glibc accepts quoted and escaped metacharacters literally and only rejects true unquoted shell separators.

Goal:
- Bring the native `wordexp` implementation into parity with host glibc for a bounded shell-lexing slice.

Deliverables:
1) Quote-aware/escape-aware bad-character validation for `wordexp`.
2) Quote-aware command-substitution detection so `WRDE_NOCMD` only rejects real command substitution.
3) Host-parity ABI tests for quoted semicolons, escaped separators, quoted command substitution literals, and bare bad-char cases.

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test wordexp_ -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:01:59.732205136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.852185122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.850227737Z","close_reason":"Match glibc wordexp quote/escape badchar and WRDE_NOCMD semantics with host-parity tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -836,7 +837,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-i3om4","title":"[HIGH] runtime_policy::ffi_pcc tests leak global state across parallel test threads","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails with:

```
thread 'runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:2168
test result: FAILED. 170 passed; 1 failed
```

Running the same test in isolation (`cargo test ... ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture`) passes cleanly: '1 passed; 0 failed; 170 filtered out'.

## Diagnosis

The test already acquires `ffi_pcc_lock()` and calls `reset_ffi_pcc_state_for_tests`, `reset_decision_contract_machine_for_tests`, `enable_runtime_kernel_for_tests`, `set_mode_state_for_tests(MODE_HARDENED)`. That pattern suggests the author was aware of test-global-state coupling. The fact that the assertion at line 2168 (`assert_eq!(decision.policy_id, FFI_PCC_POLICY_BASE + 10)`) fires only under concurrent test scheduling means **at least one of those resets does not cover all mutable state that another test modifies concurrently** — either a static/lazy cache, a thread-local, or a singleton that is not inside the lock's scope.

Adjacent tests in the same module (`ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_snprintf` et al.) use the same lock and reset helpers and presumably race on the same shared state.

## Fix direction

1. Grep for every `static ATOMIC` / `LazyLock` / `thread_local` / `Mutex<` in runtime_policy.rs that the certificate gate path touches. Classify which are covered by `ffi_pcc_lock` vs `decision_contract_machine` vs neither.
2. Any state touched by `decide()` along the `lookup_active_ffi_pcc_certificate` -> `ffi_pcc_decision` path that is not inside one of the reset-helpers is a candidate leak.
3. Confirmed repro: run the full `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` twice in a row; the failure is sometimes the 'memcpy' test, sometimes 'snprintf' or another adjacent one — classic test-ordering-dependent leak.

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace`. Part of the same full-review batch as bd-g2bd5 (glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption). Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture` passes (1/1).
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails at the same test (170/171).

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the negative-policy contract is explicit apart from the existing large-invalid-policy case.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:48.534935980Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:34.512419525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:34.510363683Z","close_reason":"Added sched_get_priority_min_negative_policy_is_einval asserting EINVAL for policy=-1.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-i7cmf","title":"FUZZ: fcntl command-matrix harness","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_fcntl.rs. Walks 22 fcntl commands (F_DUPFD, F_GETFD/SETFD, F_GETFL/SETFL, F_GETLK/SETLK/SETLKW, F_GETOWN/SETOWN/F_GETOWN_EX/SETOWN_EX, F_GETSIG/SETSIG, F_GETLEASE/SETLEASE, F_NOTIFY, F_GETPIPE_SZ/SETPIPE_SZ, F_ADD_SEALS/GET_SEALS) over a memfd scratch fd with MFD_ALLOW_SEALING. Asserts F_SETFL+F_GETFL round-trip preserves O_NONBLOCK. 7.97M runs clean at 131K exec/s.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:07:59.812712320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:10:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:10:00Z","close_reason":"Shipped: fuzz_fcntl.rs registered in Cargo.toml, builds clean, 7.97M runs at 131K exec/s over 60s, no crashes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-i8ind","title":"PORT: NUMA memory-policy family (set/get_mempolicy + mbind + migrate_pages + move_pages + set_mempolicy_home_node)","description":"Add the NUMA memory-policy family of Linux libc syscall wrappers. (1) set_mempolicy(mode, *nodemask, maxnode) -> long (SYS_set_mempolicy=238) sets the calling threads default NUMA policy. (2) get_mempolicy(*mode, *nodemask, maxnode, addr, flags) -> long (SYS_get_mempolicy=239) reads the policy for the calling thread or for a specific address. (3) mbind(addr, len, mode, *nodemask, maxnode, flags) -> long (SYS_mbind=237) binds a memory range to a NUMA policy. (4) migrate_pages(pid, maxnode, *old_nodes, *new_nodes) -> long (SYS_migrate_pages=256) migrates pages in another process between NUMA nodes. (5) move_pages(pid, count, **pages, *nodes, *status, flags) -> long (SYS_move_pages=279) moves the listed pages to specified target nodes. (6) set_mempolicy_home_node(start, len, home_node, flags) -> long (Linux 5.17+, SYS_set_mempolicy_home_node=450) sets the preferred NUMA home node for a memory range. All forward via libc::syscall; raw_syscall_with_errno bridges errno; returns are -1 on failure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:04:42.603609331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:04:42.603609331Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-i8ind","title":"PORT: NUMA memory-policy family (set/get_mempolicy + mbind + migrate_pages + move_pages + set_mempolicy_home_node)","description":"Add the NUMA memory-policy family of Linux libc syscall wrappers. (1) set_mempolicy(mode, *nodemask, maxnode) -> long (SYS_set_mempolicy=238) sets the calling threads default NUMA policy. (2) get_mempolicy(*mode, *nodemask, maxnode, addr, flags) -> long (SYS_get_mempolicy=239) reads the policy for the calling thread or for a specific address. (3) mbind(addr, len, mode, *nodemask, maxnode, flags) -> long (SYS_mbind=237) binds a memory range to a NUMA policy. (4) migrate_pages(pid, maxnode, *old_nodes, *new_nodes) -> long (SYS_migrate_pages=256) migrates pages in another process between NUMA nodes. (5) move_pages(pid, count, **pages, *nodes, *status, flags) -> long (SYS_move_pages=279) moves the listed pages to specified target nodes. (6) set_mempolicy_home_node(start, len, home_node, flags) -> long (Linux 5.17+, SYS_set_mempolicy_home_node=450) sets the preferred NUMA home node for a memory range. All forward via libc::syscall; raw_syscall_with_errno bridges errno; returns are -1 on failure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:04:42.603609331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:09:37.642841679Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:09:37.640743114Z","close_reason":"shipped: 6 NUMA memory-policy syscall wrappers + raw_syscall_with_errno_long helper + 6 abi tests including round-trip MPOL_DEFAULT and live page query via move_pages","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ibh7","title":"Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build","description":"`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. However, `scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh` only scans `*_abi.rs` files, missing `host_resolve.rs` entirely. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.\n\nTo fix:\n1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).\n2. Update `build.rs` to respect `source_scan_exceptions` so `host_resolve::dlvsym` (and any future exceptions) can be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T19:12:58.484175575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:33:37.318380234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T20:33:37.316445662Z","close_reason":"No repro; build.rs and replacement guard already align on *_abi.rs scanning and source_scan_exceptions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ibih","title":"Conformance: execute resource_ops fixture cases","description":"Extend the resource_ops conformance test so every getrlimit fixture case runs through frankenlibc_fixture_exec::execute_fixture_case and compares against expected_output. Verify with focused rch test/check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T05:30:48.010948916Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T05:36:39.776455851Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T05:36:39.774632089Z","close_reason":"Implemented executable resource_ops fixture coverage; verified with rch test/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","resource","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-id3","title":"Verification matrix: required unit/e2e/log artifacts for every critique bead","description":"Goal: make verification obligations explicit in plan space before implementation.

Deliverables:
- Machine-readable matrix keyed by bead ID with required evidence fields:
  - unit tests (commands + expected assertions)
  - e2e scripts (strict/hardened coverage)
  - structured log artifacts (schema + sample)
  - perf/isomorphism evidence where applicable
- Coverage status dashboard (missing/partial/complete).

Acceptance:
- Every open critique bead has a populated verification row.
- Missing evidence blocks bead closure.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:39:53.468918289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:00:59.835959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:00:59.835959Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","logging","testing","unit","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-id3","depends_on_id":"bd-1s7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-id3","depends_on_id":"bd-34w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-id3","depends_on_id":"bd-38s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-id3","depends_on_id":"bd-3n0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1100,"issue_id":"bd-id3","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Machine-readable verification matrix: tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json
- Covers all 57 open/in_progress critique beads

+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Mount API (statmount/listmount), module loader (finit_module), quotactl_fd,
+// map_shadow_stack, bpf, kexec_load, kexec_file_load
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+/// Linux `statmount(*req, *out, bufsize, flags) -> int` (Linux 6.8+,
+/// syscall 457) — query a single mount by ID. Both `req` and `out`
+/// are opaque kernel structs (`struct mnt_id_req` / `struct
+/// statmount`) whose layouts are version-specific.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `req` must point to a valid `struct mnt_id_req`. `out` must point
+/// to at least `bufsize` writable bytes that the kernel will fill with
+/// a `struct statmount`.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn statmount(
+    req: *const c_void,
+    out: *mut c_void,
+    bufsize: usize,
+    flags: c_uint,
+) -> c_int {
+    if req.is_null() || out.is_null() {
+        unsafe { set_abi_errno(libc::EFAULT) };
+        return -1;
+    }
+    const SYS_STATMOUNT: libc::c_long = 457;
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            SYS_STATMOUNT,
+            req as libc::c_long,
+            out as libc::c_long,
+            bufsize as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `listmount(*req, *mnt_ids, count, flags) -> int` (Linux
+/// 6.8+, syscall 458) — list child mount IDs of a mount into a
+/// `u64[count]` array.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `req` must point to a valid `struct mnt_id_req`. `mnt_ids` must
+/// point to writable storage for at least `count` `u64` entries.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn listmount(
+    req: *const c_void,
+    mnt_ids: *mut u64,
+    count: usize,
+    flags: c_uint,
+) -> c_int {
+    if req.is_null() || (count > 0 && mnt_ids.is_null()) {
+        unsafe { set_abi_errno(libc::EFAULT) };
+        return -1;
+    }
+    const SYS_LISTMOUNT: libc::c_long = 458;
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            SYS_LISTMOUNT,
+            req as libc::c_long,
+            mnt_ids as libc::c_long,
+            count as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `finit_module(fd, *param_values, flags) -> int`
+/// (`SYS_finit_module = 313`) — load a kernel module from an open
+/// file descriptor.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `param_values`, when non-NULL, must be a NUL-terminated C string
+/// of comma-separated parameter assignments.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn finit_module(
+    fd: c_int,
+    param_values: *const c_char,
+    flags: c_int,
+) -> c_int {
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_finit_module,
+            fd as libc::c_long,
+            param_values as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `quotactl_fd(fd, cmd, id, *addr) -> int` (Linux 5.14+,
+/// `SYS_quotactl_fd = 443`) — like quotactl but uses an open fd of
+/// the filesystem instead of a block-device path.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `addr` is interpreted per the supplied `cmd` (typically a
+/// pointer to a `struct dqblk`, `struct dqinfo`, etc.). When
+/// non-NULL it must point to readable/writable storage matching
+/// the cmd contract.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn quotactl_fd(
+    fd: c_uint,
+    cmd: c_int,
+    id: c_uint,
+    addr: *mut c_void,
+) -> c_int {
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_quotactl_fd,
+            fd as libc::c_long,
+            cmd as libc::c_long,
+            id as libc::c_long,
+            addr as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `map_shadow_stack(addr, size, flags) -> long` (Linux
+/// 6.6+, syscall 453) — allocate a CET shadow stack for the calling
+/// thread on x86_64. `addr == 0` lets the kernel pick.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn map_shadow_stack(addr: c_ulong, size: c_ulong, flags: c_uint) -> c_long {
+    const SYS_MAP_SHADOW_STACK: libc::c_long = 453;
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            SYS_MAP_SHADOW_STACK,
+            addr as libc::c_long,
+            size as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `bpf(cmd, *attr, size) -> int` (`SYS_bpf = 321`) —
+/// universal eBPF entry point. The interpretation of `attr`
+/// depends on `cmd` (load program, create map, lookup, ...).
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `attr` must point to at least `size` bytes describing a valid
+/// `union bpf_attr` for the requested `cmd`.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn bpf(cmd: c_int, attr: *mut c_void, size: c_uint) -> c_int {
+    if size > 0 && attr.is_null() {
+        unsafe { set_abi_errno(libc::EFAULT) };
+        return -1;
+    }
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_bpf,
+            cmd as libc::c_long,
+            attr as libc::c_long,
+            size as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `kexec_load(entry, nr_segments, *segments, flags) -> long`
+/// (`SYS_kexec_load = 246`) — load a kernel image (built up of
+/// in-memory segments) for a future `reboot(LINUX_REBOOT_CMD_KEXEC)`.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `segments`, when `nr_segments > 0`, must point to an array of
+/// `nr_segments` `struct kexec_segment` entries.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn kexec_load(
+    entry: c_ulong,
+    nr_segments: c_ulong,
+    segments: *const c_void,
+    flags: c_ulong,
+) -> c_long {
+    if nr_segments > 0 && segments.is_null() {
+        unsafe { set_abi_errno(libc::EFAULT) };
+        return -1;
+    }
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_kexec_load,
+            entry as libc::c_long,
+            nr_segments as libc::c_long,
+            segments as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
+/// Linux `kexec_file_load(kernel_fd, initrd_fd, cmdline_len,
+/// *cmdline, flags) -> long` (`SYS_kexec_file_load = 320`) — load a
+/// kernel image from `kernel_fd` (and an optional `initrd_fd`) for
+/// a future `reboot(LINUX_REBOOT_CMD_KEXEC)`.
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// `cmdline`, when `cmdline_len > 0`, must point to at least
+/// `cmdline_len` readable bytes.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn kexec_file_load(
+    kernel_fd: c_int,
+    initrd_fd: c_int,
+    cmdline_len: c_ulong,
+    cmdline: *const c_char,
+    flags: c_ulong,
+) -> c_long {
+    if cmdline_len > 0 && cmdline.is_null() {
+        unsafe { set_abi_errno(libc::EFAULT) };
+        return -1;
+    }
+    // SAFETY: forwarding to the kernel.
+    let rc = unsafe {
+        libc::syscall(
+            libc::SYS_kexec_file_load,
+            kernel_fd as libc::c_long,
+            initrd_fd as libc::c_long,
+            cmdline_len as libc::c_long,
+            cmdline as libc::c_long,
+            flags as libc::c_long,
+        )
+    };
+    unsafe { raw_syscall_with_errno_long(rc) }
+}
+
